name: Leek, Mushroom and Spinach Crepes
recipeIngredient: salt, pepper, and nutmeg to taste
recipeInstructions:
BATTER: Combine all the ingredients in a blender or mix by hand. Let rest for 2 hours. Brush crepe pan with oil. Heat pan, but do not let it get too hot. Place about half a ladle of batter in the pan and turn the pan to form a coating. The batter will stick to the pan.
Reduce heat, cook for a few minutes, then loosen the edges of the crepe with the blunt side of a knife. Lift the rest with your fingers and flip over to cook on the other side for just one minute. Repeat with the remaining batter. Alternatively, use ready-made crepe shells.
FILLING: Remove the dark green parts of the leek. Cut it in half and wash very well under cold running water. Slice it as thinly as possible (julienne). Melt butter in a pan and cook chopped onion. Add leek and mushrooms, seasoning with salt and pepper. Cook until all liquid has evaporated. Stir in flour, then cook for a few seconds. Add milk and stir until thickened. Add cream, spinach, and nutmeg, then bring to a boil. Turn off the heat.
SAUCE: Prepare the leek as for the filling. Melt butter in a pan and add leek, cooking until wilted. Season with salt and pepper. Stir in flour, cook for a few seconds, then stir in milk, stirring until it boils. Add cream and nutmeg. Set aside.
Place a heaping tablespoon of filling in the center of each crepe. Fold the edges over to form an envelope. Arrange the filled crepes in a buttered baking dish, seam side down. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 20 minutes. Reheat the sauce and spoon over the baked crepes.
